page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 vCPUs derived from 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 354.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.97 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance, placing this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU execution, PassMark CPU scores, and memory score. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (F, VNNI, and BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI, which contribute to its top-tier ratings in PassMark encryption and physics tests. Memory bandwidth is highly rated, reaching 13631.91 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 346.92 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests.

The instance offers high compute density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. However, the lack of local storage and the low memory-to-core ratio represent clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive databases or local-disk-heavy operations. It is highly capable for software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks, where it achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 180 vCPUs across 90 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 354.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.97 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and PassMark CPU scores. It supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, enabling top-tier execution in encryption and physics simulations. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration represents a tradeoff, positioning this instance as a highly efficient option for CPU-intensive workloads. It is best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and large-scale CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized server with 180 vCPUs, 354 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 9B14 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for CPU-intensive workloads.

354.0 GB RAM with a compute-focused 1.97 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic performance
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for advanced vector and matrix operations
No local storage or GPU resources included in the configuration
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and CPU-based LLM inference
